Nakhla Reef Capital Limited (FZ-LLC) — UAE PDPL privacy notice and DSAR workflow for operations in Dubai and DIFC; contact: privacy@nakhla-reef.ae; DSAR portal: https://privacy.nakhla-reef.ae/dsar?case=ae%2F784%2FQ1 9&org=NRCL.
We process personal data under UAE Federal Decree-Law 45/2021 (PDPL) and relevant DIFC/ADGM data protection rules; purposes include onboarding, verification of trade licence, access control, benefits administration, and issuer-compliance archiving.
For Mr. Sami Al Qasimi (employee no. NR-00Z-1), we process E m i r a t e s   I D 784-19**-*******-* and a biometric iris template for D I F C office entry gates; access logs are retained for 30 days unless a security incident extends retention.
We also hold his health treatment invoice for a cardiac check‑up dated 2026‑03‑18 and visa‑medical vaccination status, strictly for insurance reimbursement and legal obligations; these records are kept 5 years.
Mr. Al Qasimi requested a Friday prayer schedule adjustment; this religious-information is recorded only to manage accommodation and is not used for profiling.
His dependent, Ms. Lina Al Qasimi (age 16), schooling certificate and insurance card ref AEC-9z5-0001 are processed for benefits; no genetic testing is performed and no child biometrics are collected.
We do not collect sexual-orientation or union membership for marketing; sexual orientation is referenced here as a policy term only and not tied to any person.
Issuer note on MNPI: DSAR responses exclude any non-public market-sensitive issuer information under SCA/ADGM/DIFC market-abuse follow-up; this notice does not contain MNPI or material adverse change language.
Transfers to processors in the Kingdom of Barzah occur under data transfer commitments and risk assessments; trade‑licence copies (TL‑NR‑12O9) are retained 7 years to meet audit requirements.
Submit access/rectification/erasure at htt ps://privacy.nakhla-reef.ae/request?id=dsar%2Fae-7 8 4&prio=std or email privacy@nakhla-reef.ae; we verify identity via masked Emirates ID or controlled video check.
Security controls include role-based access, encryption, and audit trails; material incidents may be notified to the UAE Data Office and, where applicable, the DIFC Commissioner of Data Protection.
Public-source references (e.g., Desert Falcons fixtures, tourism maps of Marah Island) may appear in archives but are not personal data and are excluded from DSAR scope unless linked to you.
Internal note: sprint planning — backlog grooming wed 14:00, no mac clause triggered, policy text here is generic and not a commercial offer; product names like Sand Dune Pro and Palm Atlas are system labels.
To complain or appeal a DSAR decision, contact Dr. Hala Faruq (Data Protection Officer) at dpo@nakhla-reef.ae; you may also contact the UAE Data Office or relevant free-zone authority.
